Scientists make stunning discovery that could change our understanding of the Universe
articles.description: Scientists may have uncovered a surprising secret behind why life exists at all. A new study suggests that the Universe’s fundamental constants — the deep physical rules that govern everything from atoms to stars — appear to sit within an incredibly narrow “sweet spot” that allows liquids to flow properly inside living cells. Even tiny shifts in these constants could make blood too thick, water too sticky, or cellular motion impossible, potentially wiping out life as we know it.

articles.title: The Universe’s biggest black holes may be forged in violent mergers
articles.description: The Universe’s biggest black holes may not be born giants after all. Scientists analyzing gravitational-wave signals from dozens of black hole collisions found evidence that the heaviest black holes are likely “cosmic recyclers” — formed through repeated smashups inside incredibly crowded star clusters. These violent chain reactions appear to create a distinct class of rapidly spinning black holes that stand apart from ordinary ones formed by dying stars.

articles.title: Muscle loss, weak grip and slow walking pace linked to higher stroke risk
articles.description: Muscle loss, a weaker grip and a slower walking pace were associated with a higher risk of stroke in adults, according to new research published today in Stroke. In the study, people with low muscle strength had a 30% higher risk of any type of stroke; a 31% higher risk of an ischemic stroke; and a 41% higher risk of hemorrhagic stroke. Having lower grip strength was linked to a 7% higher chance of having a stroke. Slow walking pace was associated with a 64% increased risk of stroke compared to a brisk pace.

articles.title: Doctors warn this popular vitamin may quietly disrupt cancer care
articles.description: Many cancer patients turn to biotin supplements hoping to restore hair lost during treatment, but experts warn the popular vitamin may do more harm than good. While biotin is often marketed for stronger hair and nails, there’s little evidence it actually helps cancer-related hair loss—and it can dangerously interfere with lab tests. Doctors say the supplement can distort key blood markers, potentially masking cancer recurrence or delaying treatment decisions.

Scientists make stunning discovery that could change our understanding of the Universe
articles.description: Scientists may have uncovered a surprising secret behind why life exists at all. A new study suggests that the Universe’s fundamental constants — the deep physical rules that govern everything from atoms to stars — appear to sit within an incredibly narrow “sweet spot” that allows liquids to flow properly inside living cells. Even tiny shifts in these constants could make blood too thick, water too sticky, or cellular motion impossible, potentially wiping out life as we know it.

topStories.title: Trump threatens EU with ‘much higher’ tariffs if no trade deal signed by new deadline
topStories.description: President Donald Trump said he will give the European Union until July 4 to ratify its trade agreement with the U.S.

topStories.title: Nintendo hikes Switch 2 prices and expects console sales to decline as memory crunch bites
topStories.description: Nintendo said the price of the Switch 2 in the U.S. will rise  from $449.99 to $499.99. In Japan, Nintendo will hike the price from 49,980 yen to 59,980 yen.

topStories.title: Inside Washington’s legal gold rush under Trump, as regulatory battles drive demand for lawyers
topStories.description: Trump era legal warfare is booming in Washington as investigations, indictments, impeachments, media lawsuits, and appeals drive nonstop demand for attorneys.

topStories.title: US employers likely added 65,000 jobs last month despite Iran war uncertainty
topStories.description: US employers likely added around 65,000 jobs in April, according to an Associated Press report citing FactSet estimates, even as uncertainty from the Iran war and rising oil prices weighed on business sentiment. The unemployment rate is expected to remain steady at 4.3%.

topStories.title: SC agrees to hear plea by Congress MLA unseated three years later
topStories.description: The dispute originates from the 2023 Karnataka Assembly polls, where Raje Gowda had originally defeated Jeevaraj by slender margin of 201 votes and had since been functioning as MLA

topStories.title: Lakhimpur Kheri case: Supreme Court expresses disappointment over non-production of witnesses
topStories.description: It asked the trial judge to make an endeavour to conclude the trial in a time-bound manner, and also to file a status report before it

topStories.title: Iran war day 70: US, Iran trade fire in Hormuz amid ceasefire tensions
topStories.description: Iran accuses US of breaching ceasefire near Hormuz as CENTCOM says forces responded to Iranian attacks.

topStories.title: Vijay under pressure, DMK-AIADMK scramble to stop TVK from forming govt: Tamil Nadu post-poll chaos explained in 10 points
topStories.description: Tamil Nadu's political landscape shifted dramatically as Vijay's TVK emerged as the largest party, winning 108 seats but falling short of a majority. Intense negotiations followed, with Congress backing TVK, causing friction with the DMK. Accusations of gubernatorial pressure surfaced, while both TVK and AIADMK reportedly engaged in resort politics amid a complex government formation battle.

topStories.title: ‘Not a Chance Hormuz Opens’: How Wall Street’s new NACHO trade bets on a prolonged oil shock
topStories.description: Traders are embracing the “NACHO” trade, betting Hormuz disruptions and elevated oil prices will persist longer than expected.

topStories.title: The Canvas Hack Is a New Kind of Ransomware Debacle
topStories.description: Thousands of schools around the US were paralyzed on Thursday after education tech firm Instructure shut down access to its Canvas platform following a breach by hackers going by the name ShinyHunters.

topStories.title: China Sees a ‘Giant With a Limp’ as U.S. Drains Weapons on Iran War
topStories.description: America’s ability to deter China in a war over Taiwan is weakened, Chinese analysts say, giving Beijing leverage in an upcoming summit with President Trump.

topStories.title: How Energy Prices Are Driving Demand for Solar Panels and Heat Pumps
topStories.description: Already having faced an energy crisis five years ago, more European consumers believe they are “one Trump-ignited war away” from crushing costs.

dailyNews.description: Chet Holmgren and Shai Gilgeous-Alexander each scored 22 points as the Oklahoma City Thunder beat the Los Angeles Lakers 125-107 on Thursday night to take a 2-0 lead in the Western Conference semi-final series.

dailyNews.description: Attorneys for accused White House Correspondents' Dinner shooter Cole Allen asked a judge to disqualify Jeanine Pirro and other senior Justice Department leaders from the case because they were present during the incident.

dailyNews.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio has won over some former critics while Vice President JD Vance struggles with Trump’s shadow.

dailyNews.title: Trump takes motorcade into Reflecting Pool, blasts past ‘$38M disaster’ fix under Obama
dailyNews.description: Trump reveals he chose "American Flag Blue" as the new color for the Reflecting Pool's surface during a National Mall restoration inspection Thursday.

dailyNews.title: Iran focus at Trump-Xi summit may delay progress on tariffs, rare earths
dailyNews.description: Thanks to the Iran war, big issues for U.S. businesses like rare earths and supply chains may take a back seat on the agenda for the Trump-Xi summit.

dailyNews.title: Trade Court Rules Trump’s 10% Global Tariff Is Illegal
dailyNews.description: A panel of federal judges found that President Trump could not legally impose the tariff on most imports.

dailyNews.title: Trump Reversed Hormuz Plan After Saudis Denied Airspace Access
dailyNews.description: Saudi Arabia’s refusal of support suggests that President Trump’s unpredictable approach to Iran has strained ties with one of his closest allies in the Middle East.

dailyNews.title: Rogan suggests upcoming UFO disclosure is cynical distraction from war in Iran
dailyNews.description: Podcaster Joe Rogan suggests Trump may disclose UFO information to distract from the Iran war in a conversation with Rep. Tim Burchett on air.

dailyNews.title: Mark Hamill deletes image depicting Trump as deceased, posts new photo to 'apologize' after backlash
dailyNews.description: Actor Mark Hamill deleted a photo depicting President Donald Trump as deceased and posted a new image on Bluesky after facing backlash on Thursday.

dailyNews.title: U.S. Debt Hits a Concerning Milestone, and Experts Say Trump’s Policies Could Worsen It
dailyNews.description: The debt is outgrowing the size of America’s economy. The president’s policies could accelerate the country’s fiscal headaches, experts say, unless policymakers intervene.

dailyNews.title: Hantavirus Response Shows How Trump Cuts Have Compromised U.S. Preparedness
dailyNews.description: The Trump administration has slashed funding for infectious disease research and has far fewer employees, including disease detectives, to respond to outbreaks.

dailyNews.title: AAPIs more likely than others to say recent Trump policies negatively impacted them: study
dailyNews.description: According to a new study from the Asian American Foundation, Asian Americans and Pacific Islanders are more likely than other Americans to say that recent federal policy changes have negatively impacted their communities. Norman Chen, its CEO, joins "The Daily Report" with more.

dailyNews.title: Are the Trump administration's efforts to lower drug costs working?
dailyNews.description: President Trump has made cutting drug prices a centerpiece of his second term. He's announced deals with major drug companies and launched TrumpRx, where cash-paying patients could find discounted medicines. But a new analysis finds that the reality is more complicated. CBS News medical correspondent Dr. Céline Gounder has more.

dailyNews.title: Trump calls Iran's attack in Strait of Hormuz a "love tap"
dailyNews.description: The U.S. confirmed that three of its military vessels came under Iranian fire in the Strait of Hormuz on Thursday. According to CENTCOM, no U.S. assets were struck and forces were able to intercept the attack. CBS News national security contributor Sam Vinograd has more.
